visual_findings: The bounded one-in-five substitution placed 2400 reviewed adult broadleaf instances while retaining 80 percent of the PVE control foliage and the near-camera setback.
At guide-seat distance the substituted crowns collapse into black branch-card marks across the central and right slopes instead of creating continuous forest mass.
At river-eye distance the same dark marks become more conspicuous on the left bank and reduce depth and material continuity relative to the immutable baseline.
The three wind-sculpted coastal broadleaf meshes do not provide native Patagonian or Nothofagus species identity, sufficient canopy diversity, or stable far-distance crown behavior.
Correct rights, source hashes, physical width, grounded pivots, material roles, and Nanite configuration are necessary intake gates but do not establish a lifelike visual result.
next_asset_requirements: Acquire multiple rights-cleared native Patagonian broadleaf or reviewed Nothofagus-family species with adult crown, age, and density variants.
Require distance-stable authored foliage, impostor, or cluster behavior that preserves crown mass without opaque branch-card collapse from river cameras.
Validate leaf-card sorting, masked-material mip behavior, Nanite PreserveArea behavior, and Metal rendering before corridor-scale placement.
Author source-mask-aware forest bands, gaps, riparian transitions, understory, deadfall, wind, wetness, and seasonal states while preserving guide sightlines.
Pass ecology, guide, art, rights, gameplay, seasonal-flow, and measured desktop and VR performance review before production promotion.
